summary: Tighten the SQLite DB directory to 0o700 and chmod the DB file to 0o600 after the driver creates it.
reason: Own-audit follow-up to the Greptile cache-perms finding. The SQLite database under `~/.local/share/freshservice-pp-cli/` (or platform equivalent) contains the *primary* synced data corpus — tickets, requesters, assets, KB articles, group/department metadata — strictly more sensitive than the response cache Greptile flagged. Generator's 0o755 directory left the DB file's effective permissions to the user's umask (typically 0o644 = world-readable). Tightening dir to 0o700 and explicit chmod on the DB file closes the same exposure class.
validated_outcome: The DB directory is owner-only; the DB file itself is 0o600 after the first open. New installs are tight from creation; existing installs should `chmod -R go-rwx ~/.local/share/freshservice-pp-cli` once after upgrade.
